edibility: Premier edible oyster highly prized in cuisine; eaten fresh, dried, or cooked; cultivated globally in commercial operations. Most individuals tolerate well, though some sensitive individuals may report mild gastrointestinal effects.
taste: Mild, delicate flavor with subtle anise and seafood-like aroma; tender, velvety texture when cooked.
search: Found fall through winter in clusters on dead or dying hardwoods, stumps, and fallen logs. Identify by decurrent gills extending down the eccentric stem, fan-like cap shape, and white spore print. Key look-alikes include Angel's Wings (thinner, smaller) and Lentinellus species (more robust, toothed gills).
